violations.text: Various foods in the open air cooler were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was in unit for over 4 hours. Food was disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025

Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. PIC disposed of unknown chemical and started a new bottle. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025

The open air cooler was observed unable to maintain 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit was observed fluctuating between 38 and 46. All food inside was above 41. Unit to be used for shelf stable product only. The Gasket to the door of the walk-in was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Establishment did not have sanitizer in the establishment at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/03/2024

Food was observed held at 45-48 degrees Fahrenheit in the open air cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in there for more than 4 hours. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Open air cooler was taken out of service for storing time/temperature control for safety foods and must not be used until it is repaired/replaced/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/03/2024
